rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring left and right sides of '=' use identical types, such as bool = bool or int = int
When repairing AGREE diagnostics, avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model's configured dependencies
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must preserve type consistency; variable declarations and expressions must match scalar types (int, real, bool) as defined in the AADL model
Only use visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes; do not introduce unresolved references or synthetic identifiers
Implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes; type-level contracts should reference only visible ports and features
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE logical operators include 'and', 'or', 'not(...)', and implication '=>'; use nested 'if ... then ... else' for branching logic
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence like '0.0 -> pre(t) + step'
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only subcomponent paths or output features in component type annexes; move valid bindings to implementation scope instead
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations; instead, delete or rewrite clauses to use existing visible identifiers
irrelevant_topics: Requirement Analyst's compact semantics and traceability
Model Fusion's owner/scope and preservation rules
